intro:
# PROJECT_NAME — project handoff
This channel is the working home for the project. Below is the compressed handoff context so future work can start fast.

sections.title: 1. Project overview
sections.body: What the project is, who it serves, what problem it solves, and what the current operating goal is.

sections.title: 2. Core repos and links
sections.body: Primary repo URLs, supporting repo URLs, product links, design links, docs links, deployment links, and test URLs.

sections.title: 3. Current architecture
sections.body: Describe the stack, major flow, key services/models/APIs, and important implementation patterns.

sections.title: 4. Product / business context
sections.body: Important domain context, customer/user context, strategic intent, and key product logic that future agents or teammates need to know.

sections.title: 5. Design / UX direction
sections.body: Current design direction, UX principles, visual language choice, and what the product should feel like.

sections.title: 6. Important docs
sections.body: List the key docs, specs, strategy files, prompt libraries, and where they live.

sections.title: 7. Key milestones / decisions
sections.body: Important commits, architectural decisions, major redesigns, and notable implementation milestones.

sections.title: 8. Current status
sections.body: What is done, what is working, what is hosted, what is broken, and what still needs verification.

sections.title: 9. Priorities / next steps
sections.body: The most important next work items, in order.

sections.title: 10. Handoff rule
sections.body: Use structured context packs instead of raw dumping full chat history. Preserve the important state: overview, repos, architecture, docs, decisions, status, priorities, and links.
